modules.name: ProjectResourceView
modules.description: Resources allocated to a specific project

resources.description: Get availability for resource, params: from, to

allocations.description: Check for allocation conflicts before creating

availability_windows.description: Get availability windows, params: resource_id, from, to
Trigger background recompute for date range

flows.name: Assign Allocation
steps: POST /allocations/check-conflict with resource_id, project_id, start_date, end_date, hours_per_week
If conflict: show OverbookingAlert with overallocated dates and hours
If confirmed or no conflict: POST /allocations

flows.name: Detect Overbooking
steps: Server: for each day in range, SUM(all allocations.hours_per_week/5 + bookings.duration_hours) > resource.weekly_capacity_hours/5 * availability_pct/100
Return conflict details with overallocated dates and hours

flows.name: Capacity Planning
steps: Display CapacityHeatmap (resource vs week, color by utilization_pct)
Identify gaps and overloads from heatmap

computed_fields.formula: SUM(current allocations hours_per_week) / weekly_capacity_hours * 100 WHERE start_date<=today AND end_date>=today
SUM(allocations.hours_per_week/5 WHERE resource_id=id AND start_date<=date AND end_date>=date)
SUM(bookings.duration_hours WHERE resource_id=id AND date overlaps booking range)

domain_rules.rule: Allocation_pct cannot exceed 100% unless explicitly allowed by CapacityRule
domain_rules.ui_behavior: Show OverbookingAlert with override option if CapacityRule permits

domain_rules.rule: Resource must be active to receive allocations
domain_rules.ui_behavior: Disable allocation create button on inactive resource page

domain_rules.rule: Booking end_datetime must be after start_datetime
Allocation end_date must be after start_date
AvailabilityWindow unique per resource per date
Skill tag name unique within org

design_system.notes: Heatmap cell colors match utilization ranges. GanttTimeline uses horizontal bars per resource.

generation_rules: GanttTimeline uses virtualized rendering (e.g. react-virtual) for large resource sets
Conflict detection (check-conflict) runs server-side before allocation creation — never trust client-only validation
AvailabilityWindows are recomputed server-side after every Allocation or Booking mutation
All timestamps stored as TIMESTAMPTZ, displayed in user's local timezone derived from Resource.timezone
Soft deletes via deleted_at on all entities — add WHERE deleted_at IS NULL to all queries by default

deleted_at.notes: Soft delete — all queries filter deleted_at IS NULL

org_id_injection.middleware: req.body.org_id is stripped; injected from req.user.org_id

conflict_response.message: Record was modified by another user. Refresh and retry.

concurrency.high_contention_tables: see entities with high write concurrency

audit_trail.triggers: application-level (before write hook) — never DB trigger to avoid log bloat

standard_constraints.name: chk_{table}_deleted_order
standard_constraints.expression: deleted_at IS NULL OR deleted_at >= created_at

db_constraints_policy.enum_validation: DB ENUM types preferred over VARCHAR + CHECK; enum values defined in global enums registry

soft_delete_policy.filter: deleted_at IS NULL applied globally via query builder middleware
soft_delete_policy.cascade: Parent delete soft-deletes children via application cascade, not DB cascade
soft_delete_policy.hard_delete: Prohibited in production; available only via admin CLI with audit log
soft_delete_policy.restore: Set deleted_at = NULL; triggers audit log entry

naming_conventions.date_fields: Always use _at suffix for TIMESTAMPTZ, _date suffix for DATE
naming_conventions.id_fields: Primary key is always id; foreign keys are {entity}_id
